subject: push_to_hub on macOS
truth: push_to_hub on macOS now uses spawn instead of fork for multiprocessing, fixing segfaults but changing process spawning behaviour.
quote: This also fixes multiprocessed push_to_hub on macos that was causing segfault (now it uses spawn instead of fork).

subject: IterableDataset.push_to_hub
new: requires iterating twice to know the full dataset size
truth: IterableDataset.push_to_hub now requires iterating twice to know the full dataset size when max_shard_size is used.
quote: added max_shard_size to IterableDataset.push_to_hub (but requires iterating twice to know the full dataset twice - improvements are welcome)

from_fact: requires iterating twice to know the full dataset size

subject: Python 3.9 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.9; the library now requires Python 3.10 or newer.
quote: Drop python 3.9 by @lhoestq in https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/pull/7980

subject: load_dataset
old: invalid revision silently accepted or failed later
truth: load_dataset now raises an error early when an invalid revision is passed, instead of failing later or silently.
quote: Raise early for invalid `revision` in `load_dataset`

from_fact: invalid revision silently accepted or failed later

subject: fingerprint computation
old: TMPDIR environment variable was treated loosely and could silently fall back
new: TMPDIR is treated as a strict API and fails if invalid
truth: The fingerprint computation now treats TMPDIR as a strict API and will fail if TMPDIR is invalid, instead of silently falling back.
quote: fix(fingerprint): treat TMPDIR as strict API and fail (Issue #7877)

from_fact: tmpdir environment variable was treated loosely and could silently fall back
tmpdir is treated as a strict api and fails if invalid

subject: Parquet row group size default
truth: The default row group size for Parquet is now 100MB, which may change file structure and memory usage for code that relied on the previous default.
quote: with this change, the new default row group size for Parquet is set to 100MB

subject: Audio encoding
truth: Audio encoding now uses TorchCodec instead of Soundfile.
quote: Audio: use TorchCodec instead of Soundfile for encoding

subject: dill version
truth: dill has been bumped to version 0.4.0, which may break pickle compatibility with older dill versions.
quote: Bump dill to 0.4.0

subject: trust_remote_code
truth: Stop using trust_remote_code when loading datasets; it is no longer supported.
quote: trust_remote_code is no longer supported

subject: audio decoding backend
truth: Stop relying on soundfile for audio decoding; torchcodec is now the default audio decoder.
quote: torchcodec replaces soundfile for audio decoding

subject: video decoding backend
truth: Stop relying on decord for video decoding; torchcodec is now the default video decoder.
quote: torchcodec replaces decord for video decoding

subject: Sequence type
truth: Stop using Sequence as a feature type; use List instead. Sequence now returns List or dict depending on subfeature.
quote: Sequence was a legacy type from tensorflow datasets which converted list of dicts to dicts of lists. It is no longer a type but it becomes a utility that returns a List or a dict depending on the subfeature

subject: FolderBasedBuilder
old: FolderBasedBuilder allowed missing both data_dir and data_files
new: FolderBasedBuilder raises error when both data_dir and data_files are missing
truth: FolderBasedBuilder now raises an error when both data_dir and data_files are missing instead of silently proceeding.
quote: fix: raise error in FolderBasedBuilder when data_dir and data_files are missing

from_fact: folderbasedbuilder allowed missing both data_dir and data_files
folderbasedbuilder raises error when both data_dir and data_files are missing

subject: Python dill serialization
truth: Python 3.10+ now uses co_linetable instead of co_lnotab in _dill.py for serialization.
quote: Update `_dill.py` to use `co_linetable` for Python 3.10+ in place of `co_lnotab`

subject: Video reading backend
truth: Stop using `decord` for video reading; use `torchvision` instead. The `Video` type now returns `torchvision.io.video_reader.VideoReader` instead of a decord object.
quote: we replaced `decord` with `torchvision` to read videos, since `decord` is not maintained anymore and isn't available for recent python versions

subject: WebDataset special columns
old: WebDataset special columns were placed in any position
new: WebDataset special columns are placed in last position
truth: WebDataset special columns are now placed in the last position instead of their previous position, which can break code that assumes a specific column order.
quote: Webdataset special columns in last position

from_fact: webdataset special columns were placed in any position
webdataset special columns are placed in last position

subject: imagefolder extensions
old: .h5 files were accepted as imagefolder extensions
truth: Stop using .h5 files with imagefolder; they are no longer recognized as image extensions.
quote: Remove .h5 from imagefolder extensions

from_fact: .h5 files were accepted as imagefolder extensions

subject: Video feature - embedding behaviour
new: Videos are no longer embedded in datasets
truth: Stop relying on videos being embedded in datasets; they are now stored as references only.
quote: Don't embed videos by @lhoestq in https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/pull/7259

from_fact: videos are no longer embedded in datasets

subject: Video feature - push_to_hub
old: Video datasets could be pushed to the Hub
truth: Stop using push_to_hub on datasets with Video columns; it is now disallowed.
quote: Disallow video push_to_hub by @lhoestq in https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/pull/7265

from_fact: video datasets could be pushed to the hub

subject: dataset_info requirement
old: dataset_info was required when loading datasets
truth: Stop providing dataset_info when loading datasets; it is no longer required.
quote: No need for dataset_info by @lhoestq in https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/pull/7234

from_fact: dataset_info was required when loading datasets
